ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301103 covers oolong tea as a category; no specific entry for Lishan high-mountain oolong exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKM covers oolong tea generically; no Lishan-specific sub-code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227313005 covers oolong tea as a substance; no high-mountain or Lishan-specific concept exists.

serving.description: 1 standard brew (240 ml brewed tea from ~3 g dry leaf)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 240 ml of brewed Lishan oolong tea prepared from about 3 g of dry loose leaf, yielding a virtually calorie-free beverage.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #14355)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed oolong tea (FDC ID 171917) used as the closest approved analogue for plain brewed Lishan oolong tea. The 75 g retail weight refers to the dry loose-leaf product sold as a gift box; nutritional values are reported per 100 g of brewed tea as consumed. Dry-leaf macros are effectively zero in the brewed beverage at normal infusion ratios. Values confirmed consistent with USDA FoodData Central oolong tea brewed entry.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Beverages — Plain brewed tea (oolong, green, black): GI effectively zero; assigned GI = 1 by convention for non-glycemic beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain brewed oolong tea contains negligible carbohydrates (approximately 0.1 g per 100 ml) and has no measurable glycemic response. The University of Sydney GI Database classifies plain tea as a non-glycemic beverage. GI is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive integer) per Project Veritas zero-calorie beverage convention. Glycemic load per 240 ml serving = 0.0 g (carbohydrate content below threshold for GL calculation).

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content; polyphenols may support vascular health
hypertension.guidance: Plain brewed Lishan oolong tea contains approximately 3 mg sodium per 100 ml and no added salt, making it suitable for individuals managing hypertension; epidemiological evidence suggests regular oolong tea consumption may modestly support blood pressure regulation through its polyphenol content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate and glycemic load; polyphenols may improve insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain brewed Lishan oolong tea has a glycemic load of effectively zero per serving and contains no added sugars, making it a safe beverage choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; observational studies suggest oolong tea polyphenols may contribute to improved insulin sensitivity when consumed without added sweeteners.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol; antioxidant polyphenols (catechins, theaflavins)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain brewed Lishan oolong tea contains no saturated fat, no cholesterol, and no sodium of clinical significance, and its antioxidant polyphenols have been associated in prospective cohort studies with reduced LDL oxidation and modest cardiovascular risk reduction when consumed as part of a balanced diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lishan Oolong Tea (梨山烏龍茶) is a highly prized, premium high-mountain oolong tea originating from the Lishan (Pear Mountain) region of Taichung County, Taiwan. Cultivated at elevations between 2,000 and 2,200 metres above sea level, the tea benefits from the unique terroir of extreme altitude, cool temperatures, and persistent mist, which collectively slow the growth of the tea leaves. This slower growth concentrates aromatic compounds, resulting in a tea of exceptional complexity and depth. The leaves are meticulously hand-picked during the Spring and Winter harvest seasons. Following harvest, they undergo a precise partial oxidation process, typically ranging from 15% to 40%, which positions oolong tea uniquely between unoxidized green teas and fully oxidized black teas. This partial oxidation contributes to its distinctive flavour profile. CHICHA San Chen, a renowned Taiwanese specialty tea brand, offers Lishan Oolong in various roasting levels, each designed to highlight different facets of its character. Lighter roasts often present delicate floral notes, a fresh, buttery mouthfeel, and hints of ripe fruit, while heavier roasts develop richer, more robust profiles with toasted nut, caramel, and honey-like undertones. The brewed liquor typically displays a brilliant golden-amber hue, offering a smooth, velvety texture, a sweet, lingering aftertaste, and remarkable absence of astringency. To prepare, approximately 3-5 grams of loose leaf tea are steeped in hot water (around 90-95°C) for 1-2 minutes, often in multiple infusions, allowing the complex flavours to evolve. Nutritionally, as a plain brewed tea, Lishan oolong is virtually calorie-free (approximately 1 kcal per 100 ml), containing negligible protein, fat, and carbohydrate. It is rich in polyphenolic antioxidants, including catechins and theaflavins, which are associated with various health benefits. It also contains caffeine (approximately 30–50 mg per 240 ml serving). Clinically, plain Lishan oolong tea is considered a beneficial beverage for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease due to its effectively zero glycemic load, absence of saturated fat and cholesterol, and its potential to support vascular health and insulin sensitivity through its antioxidant compounds. It is naturally vegan, gluten-free, and halal, making it suitable for a wide range of dietary preferences. In Taiwanese tea culture, Lishan oolong is celebrated for its elegance and is often enjoyed in traditional gongfu tea ceremonies or presented as a luxurious gift.

How many calories in oolong tea?

What does Lishan oolong taste like?
Is CHICHA San Chen oolong tea healthy?

How to brew Lishan oolong tea?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for brewed oolong tea (FDC ID 171917), which is the closest approved analogue. No direct HPB Singapore or ICMR-NIN entry exists for Lishan oolong tea specifically. The 75 g retail weight refers to the dry loose-leaf product; all per-100g values are reported for the brewed beverage as consumed. GI is assigned by convention (GI = 1) as plain brewed tea contains no glycemic carbohydrates. Confidence is medium due to source adaptation rather than exact match.
notes: 1. The 75 g in the product name refers to the dry loose-leaf weight of the retail gift box, not a serving size. Nutritional values are reported per 100 g of brewed tea as consumed. 2. protein_g is naturally zero as brewed plain oolong tea contains no measurable protein at normal infusion concentrations. fat_g is naturally zero as brewed plain oolong tea contains no fat. sugar_g is confirmed zero (no added sugars; trace natural sugars below detection threshold). dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero for the brewed liquid. 3. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g per serving is below the GL calculation threshold. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; brewed tea contains no fat fractions). 5. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero; plant-derived beverage contains no B12). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero; no vitamin D in brewed tea). 6. Caffeine content is approximately 30–50 mg per 240 ml serving but is not a tracked field in the current template. 7. FoodOn URI FOODON_03301103 and FoodEx2 code A0FKM are used as closest parent matches; no Lishan-specific ontology codes exist in any of the three ontology systems as of the accessed date. 8. SNOMED CT code 227313005 verified as oolong tea substance concept. 9. The product is sold by CHICHA San Chen at One Fullerton, Singapore, at SGD 50.00 for a 75 g gift box.
